beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check tide tables and water conditions for the day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (40/100) means a gentle start; Liam wakes early as a habit to check conditions, fits his marine biology routine and tendency to plan around weather.

beats.activity: Drive along Lake Washington Boulevard toward Capitol Hill, taking the scenic route.
beats.reasoning: Liam always takes the scenic route; morning light on the water mirrors yesterday's mood-setting drive, and he values the transition time before work.

beats.activity: Arrive at lab, review sample data and prep equipment for field day on the Sound.
beats.location_name: University of Washington School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Wednesday morning routine—admin and prep before heading out on the water; consistent with his marine biology fieldwork schedule.

beats.activity: Drive to Ballard to meet field team and launch boat.
beats.reasoning: Standard fieldwork commute; mirrors Day -2 routine of meeting the team in Ballard before heading out on Puget Sound.

beats.activity: Out on Puget Sound collecting water samples, monitoring kelp forest ecosystem, documenting marine life.
beats.reasoning: Core fieldwork day; calm, clear conditions are ideal for sampling and observation; heat will drive Liam to stay hydrated and work efficiently before peak afternoon temperatures.

beats.activity: Return to shore, secure boat, grab lunch at a casual spot in Ballard.
beats.reasoning: Post-fieldwork meal near the water; Liam is hungry and the early return avoids peak heat; waterfront lunch fits his connection to the Sound.

beats.activity: Drive back to Capitol Hill lab, processing samples and entering field notes.
beats.reasoning: Return commute; data entry and lab work fills the hottest part of the day (91F peak) indoors, avoiding unnecessary heat exposure given air quality concerns.

beats.activity: Lab work: process samples, microscopy, update database.
beats.location_name: University of Washington School of Aquatic and Fishery Sciences (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Indoor work during peak heat and smoke; Liam finishes administrative tasks while conditions are harsh outside.

beats.activity: Head to climbing gym for a quick bouldering session to burn off energy.
beats.reasoning: Liam's free time passion; staying in Capitol Hill avoids an extra commute; indoor climbing avoids the lingering smoke and heat.

beats.activity: Drive home to Madrona, shower, change into casual clothes.
beats.reasoning: Return home before evening; Liam cleans up after climbing and fieldwork, settling in before the evening.

beats.activity: Light dinner at home: leftovers and water, hydrating after a hot day.
beats.reasoning: Low-key evening meal at home; Liam prioritizes hydration after heat and fieldwork; skips the crowded Gas Works concert (prefers intimate gatherings over big crowds).

beats.activity: Video call with close climbing friend to plan weekend trip, then read and relax.
beats.reasoning: Liam values time with close friends over crowds; catching up about the weekend climbing trip fits his priorities and personality, followed by low-key wind-down.
7 hours 45 minutes of sleep; ends before 06:30, allowing recovery after a full fieldwork day.

diary: Woke up early to catch the tide tables—the Sound was calling. The drive along Lake Washington Boulevard felt good, cool before the heat set in. Out on the water by mid-morning, the kelp forest was thriving, water clear despite the smoke haze from BC. Found some interesting zooplankton samples that'll keep me busy in the lab. Grabbed lunch at Ray's—nothing fancy, just needed fuel. The afternoon heat hit hard, so I ducked into the climbing gym in Capitol Hill for some bouldering. Felt good to move vertically for a bit, clear my head. Back home by evening, showered off the salt and sweat, had leftovers and a long video call with Jake about a weekend trip. The sound of that concert drifting over from Gas Works Park made me smile from my porch—everyone out there in the warmth while I was content just being still. Tired in a good way.
thought_process: Today felt balanced between the work I love and the breaks I actually need. The heat made me conscious of hydration and energy management—I knew I'd be out on the water early, so I prioritized the fieldwork before the afternoon peak. The bouldering was partly instinct: after hours in the lab and on a boat, I needed to move differently, shake off some restlessness. The video call with Jake grounded me socially without requiring me to go out into the crowds at Gas Works. The wildfire smoke from BC was in the back of my mind all day—it's a reminder that what happens up there affects us down here. I felt content staying home rather than joining the concert masses, which says something about where my head's at lately: more introspective, satisfied with smaller moments.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
